[[Category:completed]][[Category:remove category Ngoodbye $1]][[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:completed]][[Category:remove category Ngoodbye $1]][[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] <!--Ancient Japanese Census Registration System-->[[Category:Ancient Japanese Census Registration System]][[ja:古代日本の戸籍制度]]
'''Ancient Japanese Census Registration System''' (古代日本の戸籍制度, Kodai Nihon no Koseki Seido) refers to the census registration system established during the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}} in the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}} for population management. Notable examples include the {{ill|Kōgo Census|ja|庚午年籍}} (670 AD) and the {{ill|Kōin Census|ja|庚寅年籍}} (690 AD).
Some portions of these ancient census records survive as part of the {{ill|Shōsōin documents|ja|正倉院文書}}, while other records have been uncovered as {{ill|lacquered paper documents|ja|漆紙文書}} at sites such as the {{ill|Akita Castle|ja|秋田城跡}} and {{ill|Tagajō|ja|多賀城跡}}, as well as in the form of wooden tablets (木簡) in provincial locations like the {{ill|Suō Province|ja|周防国}} capital. Advances in infrared analysis have facilitated the deciphering of these records.
The Kōgo Census, compiled in 670 AD (the 9th year of Emperor Tenji's reign), marked Japan's first nationwide census. Although census records were typically destroyed after 30 years, the Kōgo Census was designated for permanent preservation. This census coincided with the enforcement of the {{ill|Ōmi Code|ja|近江令}}, reflecting its legal framework.
== The Beginnings of Census Records == Early mentions of census compilation appear in: * '''540 AD (Emperor Kinmei's reign):''' "Foreigners such as Qin and Han people, settled in the regions and listed in census registers." This indicates that census registration initially focused on managing immigrant populations.<ref>{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}</ref> * '''569 AD:''' Emperor Kinmei ordered inspections of tax registries in the {{ill|Shirai no Miyake|ja|白猪屯倉|1=Shirai no Miyake}} in the {{ill|Kibi Province|ja|吉備国}} to ensure proper registration of taxable individuals. * '''574 AD:''' {{ill|Soga no Umako|ja|蘇我馬子}} increased census efforts in Kibi, refining population control in granary-associated labor.
These efforts primarily targeted immigrants and administrative groups such as granary laborers, differing from the comprehensive population control established under the Ritsuryō system.
== Kōgo Census == The {{ill|Kōgo Census|ja|庚午年籍}} was compiled in 670 AD to implement the reforms proposed by the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化の改新}}. It represented Japan's first comprehensive national census, covering areas from {{ill|Kyūshū|ja|九州}} to {{ill|Kantō|ja|関東}}. However, the original records are no longer extant, and questions remain about whether all societal classes were included.
Subsequent records and lawsuits involving surname changes or social status disputes, however, demonstrate its use as a foundational document. The Kōgo Census was copied and stored in the central government archives until the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}.
== Kōin Census == The {{ill|Kōin Census|ja|庚寅年籍}}, compiled in 690 AD under the {{ill|Asuka Kiyomihara Code|ja|飛鳥浄御原令}}, represented the next stage in census development. It detailed all households and individuals, enabling the implementation of the {{ill|Handen-Shūju System|ja|班田収授法}} and comprehensive tax collection.
== Census Records and Reused Documents == Due to the high value of paper in ancient Japan, discarded census records were often reused by temples and government offices for other purposes. Many such reused documents, now known as {{ill|Recycled Paper Documents|ja|紙背文書}}, have been preserved in collections such as the {{ill|Shōsōin|ja|正倉院}}.
== Conclusion == The comprehensive compilation of census records continued until the mid-10th century but declined as the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}} weakened. The establishment of the {{ill|Shōen system|ja|荘園制}} reduced the need for centralized population management, marking the end of systematic census compilation.
== Related Chronology == - 540 AD: Census registration of immigrant groups. - 670 AD: Compilation of the Kōgo Census. - 690 AD: Compilation of the Kōin Census under the Asuka Kiyomihara Code. - 702 AD: The oldest surviving census, the {{ill|Hanpurī Census|ja|半布里戸籍}}, was compiled.
== References == <references/>
== Further Reading == * {{Cite journal|author=Atsushi Nito |title=Research on Shōsōin Documents |journal=National Museum of Japanese History Bulletin |date=2014-12 |volume=192 |pages=57–64 |url=https://doi.org/10.15024/00002189}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}} * {{ill|Handen-Shūju System|ja|班田収授法}} * {{ill|Ancient Japanese Provincial Administration|ja|古代日本の地方官制}} * {{ill|Census Records|ja|計帳}}

[[Category:completed]][[Category:remove category Ngoodbye $1]][[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:completed]][[Category:remove category Ngoodbye $1]][[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:completed]][[Category:remove category Ngoodbye $1]][[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:completed]][[Category:remove category Ngoodbye $1]][[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:completed]][[Category:remove category Ngoodbye $1]][[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:completed]][[Category:remove category Ngoodbye $1]][[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:New]]

[[Category:completed]][[Category:remove category Ngoodbye $1]][[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:completed]][[Category:remove category Ngoodbye $1]][[Category:Completion tier 3]] <!--Ancient Japanese Census Registration System-->[[Category:Ancient Japanese Census Registration System]][[ja:古代日本の戸籍制度]]
'''Ancient Japanese Census Registration System''' (古代日本の戸籍制度, Kodai Nihon no Koseki Seido) refers to the census registration system established during the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}} in the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}} for population management. Notable examples include the {{ill|Kōgo Census|ja|庚午年籍}} (670 AD) and the {{ill|Kōin Census|ja|庚寅年籍}} (690 AD).
Some portions of these ancient census records survive as part of the {{ill|Shōsōin documents|ja|正倉院文書}}, while other records have been uncovered as {{ill|lacquered paper documents|ja|漆紙文書}} at sites such as the {{ill|Akita Castle|ja|秋田城跡}} and {{ill|Tagajō|ja|多賀城跡}}, as well as in the form of wooden tablets (木簡) in provincial locations like the {{ill|Suō Province|ja|周防国}} capital. Advances in infrared analysis have facilitated the deciphering of these records.
The Kōgo Census, compiled in 670 AD (the 9th year of Emperor Tenji's reign), marked Japan's first nationwide census. Although census records were typically destroyed after 30 years, the Kōgo Census was designated for permanent preservation. This census coincided with the enforcement of the {{ill|Ōmi Code|ja|近江令}}, reflecting its legal framework.
== The Beginnings of Census Records == Early mentions of census compilation appear in: * '''540 AD (Emperor Kinmei's reign):''' "Foreigners such as Qin and Han people, settled in the regions and listed in census registers." This indicates that census registration initially focused on managing immigrant populations.<ref>{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}</ref> * '''569 AD:''' Emperor Kinmei ordered inspections of tax registries in the {{ill|Shirai no Miyake|ja|白猪屯倉|1=Shirai no Miyake}} in the {{ill|Kibi Province|ja|吉備国}} to ensure proper registration of taxable individuals. * '''574 AD:''' {{ill|Soga no Umako|ja|蘇我馬子}} increased census efforts in Kibi, refining population control in granary-associated labor.
These efforts primarily targeted immigrants and administrative groups such as granary laborers, differing from the comprehensive population control established under the Ritsuryō system.
== Kōgo Census == The {{ill|Kōgo Census|ja|庚午年籍}} was compiled in 670 AD to implement the reforms proposed by the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化の改新}}. It represented Japan's first comprehensive national census, covering areas from {{ill|Kyūshū|ja|九州}} to {{ill|Kantō|ja|関東}}. However, the original records are no longer extant, and questions remain about whether all societal classes were included.
Subsequent records and lawsuits involving surname changes or social status disputes, however, demonstrate its use as a foundational document. The Kōgo Census was copied and stored in the central government archives until the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}.
== Kōin Census == The {{ill|Kōin Census|ja|庚寅年籍}}, compiled in 690 AD under the {{ill|Asuka Kiyomihara Code|ja|飛鳥浄御原令}}, represented the next stage in census development. It detailed all households and individuals, enabling the implementation of the {{ill|Handen-Shūju System|ja|班田収授法}} and comprehensive tax collection.
== Census Records and Reused Documents == Due to the high value of paper in ancient Japan, discarded census records were often reused by temples and government offices for other purposes. Many such reused documents, now known as {{ill|Recycled Paper Documents|ja|紙背文書}}, have been preserved in collections such as the {{ill|Shōsōin|ja|正倉院}}.
== Conclusion == The comprehensive compilation of census records continued until the mid-10th century but declined as the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}} weakened. The establishment of the {{ill|Shōen system|ja|荘園制}} reduced the need for centralized population management, marking the end of systematic census compilation.
== Related Chronology == - 540 AD: Census registration of immigrant groups. - 670 AD: Compilation of the Kōgo Census. - 690 AD: Compilation of the Kōin Census under the Asuka Kiyomihara Code. - 702 AD: The oldest surviving census, the {{ill|Hanpurī Census|ja|半布里戸籍}}, was compiled.
== References == <references/>
== Further Reading == * {{Cite journal|author=Atsushi Nito |title=Research on Shōsōin Documents |journal=National Museum of Japanese History Bulletin |date=2014-12 |volume=192 |pages=57–64 |url=https://doi.org/10.15024/00002189}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}} * {{ill|Handen-Shūju System|ja|班田収授法}} * {{ill|Ancient Japanese Provincial Administration|ja|古代日本の地方官制}} * {{ill|Census Records|ja|計帳}}

The Kōgo Census, compiled in 670 AD (the 9th year of Emperor Tenji's reign), marked Japan's first nationwide census. Although census records were typically destroyed after 30 years, the Kōgo Census was designated for permanent preservation. This census coincided with the enforcement of the {{ill|Ōmi Code|ja|近江令}}, reflecting its legal framework.
== The Beginnings of Census Records == Early mentions of census compilation appear in: * '''540 AD (Emperor Kinmei's reign):''' "Foreigners such as Qin and Han people, settled in the regions and listed in census registers." This indicates that census registration initially focused on managing immigrant populations.<ref>{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}</ref> * '''569 AD:''' Emperor Kinmei ordered inspections of tax registries in the {{ill|Shirai no Miyake|ja|白猪屯倉|1=Shirai no Miyake}} in the {{ill|Kibi Province|ja|吉備国}} to ensure proper registration of taxable individuals. * '''574 AD:''' {{ill|Soga no Umako|ja|蘇我馬子}} increased census efforts in Kibi, refining population control in granary-associated labor.
These efforts primarily targeted immigrants and administrative groups such as granary laborers, differing from the comprehensive population control established under the Ritsuryō system.
== Kōgo Census == The {{ill|Kōgo Census|ja|庚午年籍}} was compiled in 670 AD to implement the reforms proposed by the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化の改新}}. It represented Japan's first comprehensive national census, covering areas from {{ill|Kyūshū|ja|九州}} to {{ill|Kantō|ja|関東}}. However, the original records are no longer extant, and questions remain about whether all societal classes were included.
Subsequent records and lawsuits involving surname changes or social status disputes, however, demonstrate its use as a foundational document. The Kōgo Census was copied and stored in the central government archives until the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}.
== Kōin Census == The {{ill|Kōin Census|ja|庚寅年籍}}, compiled in 690 AD under the {{ill|Asuka Kiyomihara Code|ja|飛鳥浄御原令}}, represented the next stage in census development. It detailed all households and individuals, enabling the implementation of the {{ill|Handen-Shūju System|ja|班田収授法}} and comprehensive tax collection.
== Census Records and Reused Documents == Due to the high value of paper in ancient Japan, discarded census records were often reused by temples and government offices for other purposes. Many such reused documents, now known as {{ill|Recycled Paper Documents|ja|紙背文書}}, have been preserved in collections such as the {{ill|Shōsōin|ja|正倉院}}.
== Conclusion == The comprehensive compilation of census records continued until the mid-10th century but declined as the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}} weakened. The establishment of the {{ill|Shōen system|ja|荘園制}} reduced the need for centralized population management, marking the end of systematic census compilation.
== Related Chronology == - 540 AD: Census registration of immigrant groups. - 670 AD: Compilation of the Kōgo Census. - 690 AD: Compilation of the Kōin Census under the Asuka Kiyomihara Code. - 702 AD: The oldest surviving census, the {{ill|Hanpurī Census|ja|半布里戸籍}}, was compiled.
== References == <references/>
== Further Reading == * {{Cite journal|author=Atsushi Nito |title=Research on Shōsōin Documents |journal=National Museum of Japanese History Bulletin |date=2014-12 |volume=192 |pages=57–64 |url=https://doi.org/10.15024/00002189}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}} * {{ill|Handen-Shūju System|ja|班田収授法}} * {{ill|Ancient Japanese Provincial Administration|ja|古代日本の地方官制}} * {{ill|Census Records|ja|計帳}}

[[Category:completed]][[Category:remove category Ngoodbye $1]][[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category: tier 3]] [[Category:completed]][[Category:remove category Ngoodbye $1]][[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category: tier 3]] <!--Ancient Japanese Census Registration System-->[[Category:Ancient Japanese Census Registration System]][[ja:古代日本の戸籍制度]]
'''Ancient Japanese Census Registration System''' (古代日本の戸籍制度, Kodai Nihon no Koseki Seido) refers to the census registration system established during the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}} in the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}} for population management. Notable examples include the {{ill|Kōgo Census|ja|庚午年籍}} (670 AD) and the {{ill|Kōin Census|ja|庚寅年籍}} (690 AD).
Some portions of these ancient census records survive as part of the {{ill|Shōsōin documents|ja|正倉院文書}}, while other records have been uncovered as {{ill|lacquered paper documents|ja|漆紙文書}} at sites such as the {{ill|Akita Castle|ja|秋田城跡}} and {{ill|Tagajō|ja|多賀城跡}}, as well as in the form of wooden tablets (木簡) in provincial locations like the {{ill|Suō Province|ja|周防国}} capital. Advances in infrared analysis have facilitated the deciphering of these records.
The Kōgo Census, compiled in 670 AD (the 9th year of Emperor Tenji's reign), marked Japan's first nationwide census. Although census records were typically destroyed after 30 years, the Kōgo Census was designated for permanent preservation. This census coincided with the enforcement of the {{ill|Ōmi Code|ja|近江令}}, reflecting its legal framework.

This indicates that census registration initially focused on managing immigrant populations.<ref>{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}</ref> * '''569 AD:''' Emperor Kinmei ordered inspections of tax registries in the {{ill|Shirai no Miyake|ja|白猪屯倉|1=Shirai no Miyake}} in the {{ill|Kibi Province|ja|吉備国}} to ensure proper registration of taxable individuals. * '''574 AD:''' {{ill|Soga no Umako|ja|蘇我馬子}} increased census efforts in Kibi, refining population control in granary-associated labor.
These efforts primarily targeted immigrants and administrative groups such as granary laborers, differing from the comprehensive population control established under the Ritsuryō system.
== Kōgo Census == The {{ill|Kōgo Census|ja|庚午年籍}} was compiled in 670 AD to implement the reforms proposed by the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化の改新}}. It represented Japan's first comprehensive national census, covering areas from {{ill|Kyūshū|ja|九州}} to {{ill|Kantō|ja|関東}}. However, the original records are no longer extant, and questions remain about whether all societal classes were included.
Subsequent records and lawsuits involving surname changes or social status disputes, however, demonstrate its use as a foundational document. The Kōgo Census was copied and stored in the central government archives until the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}.
== Kōin Census == The {{ill|Kōin Census|ja|庚寅年籍}}, compiled in 690 AD under the {{ill|Asuka Kiyomihara Code|ja|飛鳥浄御原令}}, represented the next stage in census development. It detailed all households and individuals, enabling the implementation of the {{ill|Handen-Shūju System|ja|班田収授法}} and comprehensive tax collection.
== Census Records and Reused Documents == Due to the high value of paper in ancient Japan, discarded census records were often reused by temples and government offices for other purposes. Many such reused documents, now known as {{ill|Recycled Paper Documents|ja|紙背文書}}, have been preserved in collections such as the {{ill|Shōsōin|ja|正倉院}}.
== Conclusion == The comprehensive compilation of census records continued until the mid-10th century but declined as the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}} weakened. The establishment of the {{ill|Shōen system|ja|荘園制}} reduced the need for centralized population management, marking the end of systematic census compilation.
== Related Chronology == - 540 AD: Census registration of immigrant groups. - 670 AD: Compilation of the Kōgo Census. - 690 AD: Compilation of the Kōin Census under the Asuka Kiyomihara Code. - 702 AD: The oldest surviving census, the {{ill|Hanpurī Census|ja|半布里戸籍}}, was compiled.
== References == <references/>
== Further Reading == * {{Cite journal|author=Atsushi Nito |title=Research on Shōsōin Documents |journal=National Museum of Japanese History Bulletin |date=2014-12 |volume=192 |pages=57–64 |url=https://doi.org/10.15024/00002189}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}} * {{ill|Handen-Shūju System|ja|班田収授法}} * {{ill|Ancient Japanese Provincial Administration|ja|古代日本の地方官制}} * {{ill|Census Records|ja|計帳}}

This indicates that census registration initially focused on managing immigrant populations.<ref>{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}</ref> * '''569 AD:''' Emperor Kinmei ordered inspections of tax registries in the {{ill|Shirai no Miyake|ja|白猪屯倉|1=Shirai no Miyake}} in the {{ill|Kibi Province|ja|吉備国}} to ensure proper registration of taxable individuals. * '''574 AD:''' {{ill|Soga no Umako|ja|蘇我馬子}} increased census efforts in Kibi, refining population control in granary-associated labor. These efforts primarily targeted immigrants and administrative groups such as granary laborers, differing from the comprehensive population control established under the Ritsuryō system. == Kōgo Census == The {{ill|Kōgo Census|ja|庚午年籍}} was compiled in 670 AD to implement the reforms proposed by the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化の改新}}. It represented Japan's first comprehensive national census, covering areas from {{ill|Kyūshū|ja|九州}} to {{ill|Kantō|ja|関東}}. However, the original records are no longer extant, and questions remain about whether all societal classes were included. Subsequent records and lawsuits involving surname changes or social status disputes, however, demonstrate its use as a foundational document. The Kōgo Census was copied and stored in the central government archives until the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}. == Kōin Census == The {{ill|Kōin Census|ja|庚寅年籍}}, compiled in 690 AD under the {{ill|Asuka Kiyomihara Code|ja|飛鳥浄御原令}}, represented the next stage in census development. It detailed all households and individuals, enabling the implementation of the {{ill|Handen-Shūju System|ja|班田収授法}} and comprehensive tax collection. == Census Records and Reused Documents == Due to the high value of paper in ancient Japan, discarded census records were often reused by temples and government offices for other purposes. Many such reused documents, now known as {{ill|Recycled Paper Documents|ja|紙背文書}}, have been preserved in collections such as the {{ill|Shōsōin|ja|正倉院}}. == Conclusion == The comprehensive compilation of census records continued until the mid-10th century but declined as the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}} weakened. The establishment of the {{ill|Shōen system|ja|荘園制}} reduced the need for centralized population management, marking the end of systematic census compilation. == Related Chronology == - 540 AD: Census registration of immigrant groups. - 670 AD: Compilation of the Kōgo Census. - 690 AD: Compilation of the Kōin Census under the Asuka Kiyomihara Code. - 702 AD: The oldest surviving census, the {{ill|Hanpurī Census|ja|半布里戸籍}}, was compiled. == References == <references/> == Further Reading == * {{Cite journal|author=Atsushi Nito |title=Research on Shōsōin Documents |journal=National Museum of Japanese History Bulletin |date=2014-12 |volume=192 |pages=57–64 |url=https://doi.org/10.15024/00002189}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}} * {{ill|Handen-Shūju System|ja|班田収授法}} * {{ill|Ancient Japanese Provincial Administration|ja|古代日本の地方官制}} * {{ill|Census Records|ja|計帳}}

It detailed all households and individuals, enabling the implementation of the {{ill|Handen-Shūju System|ja|班田収授法|12=draft}} and comprehensive tax collection. == Census Records and Reused Documents == Due to the high value of paper in ancient Japan, discarded census records were often reused by temples and government offices for other purposes. Many such reused documents, now known as {{ill|Recycled Paper Documents|ja|紙背文書|12=draft}}, have been preserved in collections such as the {{ill|Shōsōin|ja|正倉院|12=draft}}. == Conclusion == The comprehensive compilation of census records continued until the mid-10th century but declined as the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制|12=draft}} weakened. The establishment of the {{ill|Shōen system|ja|荘園制|12=draft}} reduced the need for centralized population management, marking the end of systematic census compilation. == Related Chronology == - 540 AD: Census registration of immigrant groups. - 670 AD: Compilation of the Kōgo Census. - 690 AD: Compilation of the Kōin Census under the Asuka Kiyomihara Code. - 702 AD: The oldest surviving census, the {{ill|Hanpurī Census|ja|半布里戸籍|12=draft}}, was compiled. == References == <references/> == Further Reading == * {{Cite journal|author=Atsushi Nito |title=Research on Shōsōin Documents |journal=National Museum of Japanese History Bulletin |date=2014-12 |volume=192 |pages=57–64 |url=https://doi.org/10.15024/00002189}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制|12=draft}} * {{ill|Handen-Shūju System|ja|班田収授法|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ancient Japanese Provincial Administration|ja|古代日本の地方官制|12=draft}} * {{ill|Census Records|ja|計帳|12=draft}}

revision.text:
<!--Ancient Japanese Census Registration System--> '''Ancient Japanese Census Registration System''' (古代日本の戸籍制度, Kodai Nihon no Koseki Seido) refers to the census registration system established during the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制|12=draft}} in the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代|12=draft}} for population management. Notable examples include the {{ill|Kōgo Census|ja|庚午年籍|12=draft}} (670 AD) and the {{ill|Kōin Census|ja|庚寅年籍|12=draft}} (690 AD). Some portions of these ancient census records survive as part of the {{ill|Shōsōin documents|ja|正倉院文書|12=draft}}, while other records have been uncovered as {{ill|lacquered paper documents|ja|漆紙文書|12=draft}} at sites such as the {{ill|Akita Castle|ja|秋田城跡|12=draft}} and {{ill|Tagajō|ja|多賀城跡|12=draft}}, as well as in the form of wooden tablets (木簡) in provincial locations like the {{ill|Suō Province|ja|周防国|12=draft}} capital. Advances in infrared analysis have facilitated the deciphering of these records. The Kōgo Census, compiled in 670 AD (the 9th year of Emperor Tenji's reign), marked Japan's first nationwide census. Although census records were typically destroyed after 30 years, the Kōgo Census was designated for permanent preservation. This census coincided with the enforcement of the {{ill|Ōmi Code|ja|近江令|12=draft}}, reflecting its legal framework. == The Beginnings of Census Records == Early mentions of census compilation appear in: * '''540 AD (Emperor Kinmei's reign):''' "Foreigners such as Qin and Han people, settled in the regions and listed in census registers." This indicates that census registration initially focused on managing immigrant populations.<ref>{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}</ref> * '''569 AD:''' Emperor Kinmei ordered inspections of tax registries in the {{ill|Shirai no Miyake|ja|白猪屯倉|1=Shirai no Miyake|12=draft}} in the {{ill|Kibi Province|ja|吉備国|12=draft}} to ensure proper registration of taxable individuals. * '''574 AD:''' {{ill|Soga no Umako|ja|蘇我馬子|12=draft}} increased census efforts in Kibi, refining population control in granary-associated labor. These efforts primarily targeted immigrants and administrative groups such as granary laborers, differing from the comprehensive population control established under the Ritsuryō system. == Kōgo Census == The {{ill|Kōgo Census|ja|庚午年籍|12=draft}} was compiled in 670 AD to implement the reforms proposed by the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化の改新|12=draft}}. It represented Japan's first comprehensive national census, covering areas from {{ill|Kyūshū|ja|九州|12=draft}} to {{ill|Kantō|ja|関東|12=draft}}. However, the original records are no longer extant, and questions remain about whether all societal classes were included. Subsequent records and lawsuits involving surname changes or social status disputes, however, demonstrate its use as a foundational document. The Kōgo Census was copied and stored in the central government archives until the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft}}. == Kōin Census == The {{ill|Kōin Census|ja|庚寅年籍|12=draft}}, compiled in 690 AD under the {{ill|Asuka Kiyomihara Code|ja|飛鳥浄御原令|12=draft}}, represented the next stage in census development. It detailed all households and individuals, enabling the implementation of the {{ill|Handen-Shūju System|ja|班田収授法|12=draft}} and comprehensive tax collection. == Census Records and Reused Documents == Due to the high value of paper in ancient Japan, discarded census records were often reused by temples and government offices for other purposes. Many such reused documents, now known as {{ill|Recycled Paper Documents|ja|紙背文書|12=draft}}, have been preserved in collections such as the {{ill|Shōsōin|ja|正倉院|12=draft}}. == Conclusion == The comprehensive compilation of census records continued until the mid-10th century but declined as the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制|12=draft}} weakened. The establishment of the {{ill|Shōen system|ja|荘園制|12=draft}} reduced the need for centralized population management, marking the end of systematic census compilation. == Related Chronology == - 540 AD: Census registration of immigrant groups. - 670 AD: Compilation of the Kōgo Census. - 690 AD: Compilation of the Kōin Census under the Asuka Kiyomihara Code. - 702 AD: The oldest surviving census, the {{ill|Hanpurī Census|ja|半布里戸籍|12=draft}}, was compiled. == References == <references/> == Further Reading == * {{Cite journal|author=Atsushi Nito |title=Research on Shōsōin Documents |journal=National Museum of Japanese History Bulletin |date=2014-12 |volume=192 |pages=57–64 |url=https://doi.org/10.15024/00002189}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制|12=draft}} * {{ill|Handen-Shūju System|ja|班田収授法|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ancient Japanese Provincial Administration|ja|古代日本の地方官制|12=draft}} * {{ill|Census Records|ja|計帳|12=draft}}

These efforts primarily targeted immigrants and administrative groups such as granary laborers, differing from the comprehensive population control established under the Ritsuryō system. == Kōgo Census == The {{ill|Kōgo Census|ja|庚午年籍|12=draft|12=draft}} was compiled in 670 AD to implement the reforms proposed by the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化の改新|12=draft|12=draft}}. It represented Japan's first comprehensive national census, covering areas from {{ill|Kyūshū|ja|九州|12=draft|12=draft}} to {{ill|Kantō|ja|関東|12=draft|12=draft}}. However, the original records are no longer extant, and questions remain about whether all societal classes were included. Subsequent records and lawsuits involving surname changes or social status disputes, however, demonstrate its use as a foundational document. The Kōgo Census was copied and stored in the central government archives until the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Kōin Census == The {{ill|Kōin Census|ja|庚寅年籍|12=draft|12=draft}}, compiled in 690 AD under the {{ill|Asuka Kiyomihara Code|ja|飛鳥浄御原令|12=draft|12=draft}}, represented the next stage in census development. It detailed all households and individuals, enabling the implementation of the {{ill|Handen-Shūju System|ja|班田収授法|12=draft|12=draft}} and comprehensive tax collection. == Census Records and Reused Documents == Due to the high value of paper in ancient Japan, discarded census records were often reused by temples and government offices for other purposes. Many such reused documents, now known as {{ill|Recycled Paper Documents|ja|紙背文書|12=draft|12=draft}}, have been preserved in collections such as the {{ill|Shōsōin|ja|正倉院|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Conclusion == The comprehensive compilation of census records continued until the mid-10th century but declined as the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制|12=draft|12=draft}} weakened. The establishment of the {{ill|Shōen system|ja|荘園制|12=draft|12=draft}} reduced the need for centralized population management, marking the end of systematic census compilation. == Related Chronology == - 540 AD: Census registration of immigrant groups. - 670 AD: Compilation of the Kōgo Census. - 690 AD: Compilation of the Kōin Census under the Asuka Kiyomihara Code. - 702 AD: The oldest surviving census, the {{ill|Hanpurī Census|ja|半布里戸籍|12=draft|12=draft}}, was compiled. == References == <references/> == Further Reading == * {{Cite journal|author=Atsushi Nito |title=Research on Shōsōin Documents |journal=National Museum of Japanese History Bulletin |date=2014-12 |volume=192 |pages=57–64 |url=https://doi.org/10.15024/00002189}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Handen-Shūju System|ja|班田収授法|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ancient Japanese Provincial Administration|ja|古代日本の地方官制|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Census Records|ja|計帳|12=draft|12=draft}}

<!--Ancient Japanese Census Registration System--> '''Ancient Japanese Census Registration System''' (古代日本の戸籍制度, Kodai Nihon no Koseki Seido) refers to the census registration system established during the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制|12=draft|12=draft}} in the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代|12=draft|12=draft}} for population management. Notable examples include the {{ill|Kōgo Census|ja|庚午年籍|12=draft|12=draft}} (670 AD) and the {{ill|Kōin Census|ja|庚寅年籍|12=draft|12=draft}} (690 AD). Some portions of these ancient census records survive as part of the {{ill|Shōsōin documents|ja|正倉院文書|12=draft|12=draft}}, while other records have been uncovered as {{ill|lacquered paper documents|ja|漆紙文書|12=draft|12=draft}} at sites such as the {{ill|Akita Castle|ja|秋田城跡|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Tagajō|ja|多賀城跡|12=draft|12=draft}}, as well as in the form of wooden tablets (木簡) in provincial locations like the {{ill|Suō Province|ja|周防国|12=draft|12=draft}} capital. Although census records were typically destroyed after 30 years, the Kōgo Census was designated for permanent preservation. This census coincided with the enforcement of the {{ill|Ōmi Code|ja|近江令|12=draft|12=draft}}, reflecting its legal framework. == The Beginnings of Census Records == Early mentions of census compilation appear in: * '''540 AD (Emperor Kinmei's reign):''' "Foreigners such as Qin and Han people, settled in the regions and listed in census registers." However, the original records are no longer extant, and questions remain about whether all societal classes were included. Subsequent records and lawsuits involving surname changes or social status disputes, however, demonstrate its use as a foundational document. The Kōgo Census was copied and stored in the central government archives until the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Kōin Census == The {{ill|Kōin Census|ja|庚寅年籍|12=draft|12=draft}}, compiled in 690 AD under the {{ill|Asuka Kiyomihara Code|ja|飛鳥浄御原令|12=draft|12=draft}}, represented the next stage in census development. It detailed all households and individuals, enabling the implementation of the {{ill|Handen-Shūju System|ja|班田収授法|12=draft|12=draft}} and comprehensive tax collection. == Census Records and Reused Documents == Due to the high value of paper in ancient Japan, discarded census records were often reused by temples and government offices for other purposes. <!--Ancient Japanese Census Registration System--> '''Ancient Japanese Census Registration System''' (古代日本の戸籍制度, Kodai Nihon no Koseki Seido) refers to the census registration system established during the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制|12=draft|12=draft}} in the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代|12=draft|12=draft}} for population management. Notable examples include the {{ill|Kōgo Census|ja|庚午年籍|12=draft|12=draft}} (670 AD) and the {{ill|Kōin Census|ja|庚寅年籍|12=draft|12=draft}} (690 AD). Some portions of these ancient census records survive as part of the {{ill|Shōsōin documents|ja|正倉院文書|12=draft|12=draft}}, while other records have been uncovered as {{ill|lacquered paper documents|ja|漆紙文書|12=draft|12=draft}} at sites such as the {{ill|Akita Castle|ja|秋田城跡|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Tagajō|ja|多賀城跡|12=draft|12=draft}}, as well as in the form of wooden tablets (木簡) in provincial locations like the {{ill|Suō Province|ja|周防国|12=draft|12=draft}} capital. Advances in infrared analysis have facilitated the deciphering of these records. The Kōgo Census, compiled in 670 AD (the 9th year of Emperor Tenji's reign), marked Japan's first nationwide census. Although census records were typically destroyed after 30 years, the Kōgo Census was designated for permanent preservation. This census coincided with the enforcement of the {{ill|Ōmi Code|ja|近江令|12=draft|12=draft}}, reflecting its legal framework. == The Beginnings of Census Records == Early mentions of census compilation appear in: * '''540 AD (Emperor Kinmei's reign):''' "Foreigners such as Qin and Han people, settled in the regions and listed in census registers." This indicates that census registration initially focused on managing immigrant populations.<ref>{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}}</ref> * '''569 AD:''' Emperor Kinmei ordered inspections of tax registries in the {{ill|Shirai no Miyake|ja|白猪屯倉|1=Shirai no Miyake|12=draft|12=draft}} in the {{ill|Kibi Province|ja|吉備国|12=draft|12=draft}} to ensure proper registration of taxable individuals. * '''574 AD:''' {{ill|Soga no Umako|ja|蘇我馬子|12=draft|12=draft}} increased census efforts in Kibi, refining population control in granary-associated labor. These efforts primarily targeted immigrants and administrative groups such as granary laborers, differing from the comprehensive population control established under the Ritsuryō system. == Kōgo Census == The {{ill|Kōgo Census|ja|庚午年籍|12=draft|12=draft}} was compiled in 670 AD to implement the reforms proposed by the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化の改新|12=draft|12=draft}}. It represented Japan's first comprehensive national census, covering areas from {{ill|Kyūshū|ja|九州|12=draft|12=draft}} to {{ill|Kantō|ja|関東|12=draft|12=draft}}. However, the original records are no longer extant, and questions remain about whether all societal classes were included. Subsequent records and lawsuits involving surname changes or social status disputes, however, demonstrate its use as a foundational document. The Kōgo Census was copied and stored in the central government archives until the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Kōin Census == The {{ill|Kōin Census|ja|庚寅年籍|12=draft|12=draft}}, compiled in 690 AD under the {{ill|Asuka Kiyomihara Code|ja|飛鳥浄御原令|12=draft|12=draft}}, represented the next stage in census development. It detailed all households and individuals, enabling the implementation of the {{ill|Handen-Shūju System|ja|班田収授法|12=draft|12=draft}} and comprehensive tax collection. == Census Records and Reused Documents == Due to the high value of paper in ancient Japan, discarded census records were often reused by temples and government offices for other purposes. Many such reused documents, now known as {{ill|Recycled Paper Documents|ja|紙背文書|12=draft|12=draft}}, have been preserved in collections such as the {{ill|Shōsōin|ja|正倉院|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Conclusion == The comprehensive compilation of census records continued until the mid-10th century but declined as the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制|12=draft|12=draft}} weakened. The establishment of the {{ill|Shōen system|ja|荘園制|12=draft|12=draft}} reduced the need for centralized population management, marking the end of systematic census compilation. == Related Chronology == - 540 AD: Census registration of immigrant groups. - 670 AD: Compilation of the Kōgo Census. - 690 AD: Compilation of the Kōin Census under the Asuka Kiyomihara Code. - 702 AD: The oldest surviving census, the {{ill|Hanpurī Census|ja|半布里戸籍|12=draft|12=draft}}, was compiled. == References == <references/> == Further Reading == * {{Cite journal|author=Atsushi Nito |title=Research on Shōsōin Documents |journal=National Museum of Japanese History Bulletin |date=2014-12 |volume=192 |pages=57–64 |url=https://doi.org/10.15024/00002189}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Handen-Shūju System|ja|班田収授法|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ancient Japanese Provincial Administration|ja|古代日本の地方官制|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Census Records|ja|計帳|12=draft|12=draft}} <!--Ancient Japanese Census Registration System--> <!--Ancient Japanese Census Registration System--> {{draft categories|}}
{{draft categories|[[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese Census Registration System]] [[Category:Ritsuryō system]] [[Category:Asuka period]] [[Category:Nara period]] [[Category:Census]] }} [[ja:古代日本の戸籍制度]]

It detailed all households and individuals, enabling the implementation of the {{ill|Handen-Shūju System|ja|班田収授法|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} and comprehensive tax collection. == Census Records and Reused Documents == Due to the high value of paper in ancient Japan, discarded census records were often reused by temples and government offices for other purposes. Many such reused documents, now known as {{ill|Recycled Paper Documents|ja|紙背文書|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, have been preserved in collections such as the {{ill|Shōsōin|ja|正倉院|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Conclusion == The comprehensive compilation of census records continued until the mid-10th century but declined as the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} weakened. The establishment of the {{ill|Shōen system|ja|荘園制|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} reduced the need for centralized population management, marking the end of systematic census compilation. == Related Chronology == - 540 AD: Census registration of immigrant groups. - 670 AD: Compilation of the Kōgo Census. - 690 AD: Compilation of the Kōin Census under the Asuka Kiyomihara Code. - 702 AD: The oldest surviving census, the {{ill|Hanpurī Census|ja|半布里戸籍|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, was compiled. == References == <references/> == Further Reading == * {{Cite journal|author=Atsushi Nito |title=Research on Shōsōin Documents |journal=National Museum of Japanese History Bulletin |date=2014-12 |volume=192 |pages=57–64 |url=https://doi.org/10.15024/00002189}} == See Also == * {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Handen-Shūju System|ja|班田収授法|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ancient Japanese Provincial Administration|ja|古代日本の地方官制|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Census Records|ja|計帳|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese Census Registration System]] [[Category:Ritsuryō system]] [[Category:Asuka period]] [[Category:Nara period]] [[Category:Census]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ancient Japanese Census Registration System]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:古代日本の戸籍制度]] <!--Ancient Japanese Census Registration System--> <!--Ancient Japanese Census Registration System--> <!--Ancient Japanese Census Registration System-->

revision.comment: 103 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:古代日本の戸籍制度 up to rev 103916794
revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2011-9}} '''古代日本の戸籍制度'''（こだいにほんのこせきせいど）は、[[飛鳥時代]]に撰定・編纂された[[律令]]による人民把握のための[[戸籍]]。主なものに[[古代日本の戸籍制度#庚午年籍|庚午年籍]]（こうごのねんじゃく）や[[古代日本の戸籍制度#庚寅年籍|庚寅年籍]]（こういんのねんじゃく）があげられる。
[[正倉院文書]]に古代の戸籍の一部が<!---[[死亡帳]]などとして、--->残されている。また近年、[[漆紙文書]]のかたちで[[秋田城|秋田城跡]]や[[多賀城|多賀城跡]]、[[下野国|下野]][[国府]]跡など地方の[[城柵|城柵遺跡]]や[[官衙]]から、戸籍[[木簡]]としては[[周防国|周防]]国府などで出土しており、[[赤外線]]による解読作業がおこなわれている。
このうち庚午年籍は、670年（天智9年／庚午の年）につくられた戸籍。古代においては、一般の戸籍は6年ごとに作成され、30年を経ると廃棄される規定であったが、庚午年籍は永久保存とされた。しかも、この年が『近江令』施行の年でもあったから、これにならってつくられたものである。
==戸籍のはじまり== 造籍に関する古い例としては、
[[540年]]（[[欽明天皇|欽明]]元年）八月の条「秦人（はたひと）・漢人（あやひと）等、諸蕃（となりのくに）より投化せる者を召し集へて、国郡に安置し、戸籍（へのふみた）に編貫す。秦人の戸数七千五十三戸、大蔵掾（おおくらのふびと）を以て、秦伴造（はたのとものみやつこ）となす」（『[[日本書紀]]』）。とあり、欽明朝頃にはまず[[渡来人]]を戸籍によって支配したことが窺われる。
[[569年]]（欽明30）の春正月に、詔で[[吉備国|吉備]]の[[白猪屯倉]]（しらいのみやけ）では、年齢が十歳あまりに達しているのに、籍に漏れているために賦課を免ぜられている者が多い。[[白猪胆津|膽津]]（いつ）を遣わして[[田部]]の丁籍を検定せよと述べた。4月になって、膽津は詔に述べられているとおりによく丁（よほろ）を調査して籍を定め、田戸を編成したので、その功をほめて白猪史（しらいのふひと）の姓を賜い、[[田令 (屯倉)|田令]]（たづかい）に任じた（『日本書紀』）。丁籍は、課役を負担する成年男子のみを記載した。田戸は、田部を編成して丁籍よりも正確な戸籍を造ったのか。はじめに籍を造っただけで、後は定期的に籍を作成することもなかったらしいので、このような不具合が生じたらしい。
さらに[[574年]]（[[敏達天皇|敏達]]3）十月の条に、[[大臣 (古代日本)|大臣]]の[[蘇我馬子]]を吉備に遣わし、白猪屯倉と田部とを増益して、その田部の名籍を膽津に授けたとある（『日本書紀』）。
名籍は、胆津が新しく造ったもので、後の戸籍・計帳に近いものか。これらは渡来系集団や屯倉の田部などの造籍であり、すべての人民を対象とする[[律令制]]の戸籍制とは異なる。
== 庚午年籍 == [[646年]]（大化2）[[改新の詔]]を発布して今後の政治改革の方針を示した（しかし、今日では改新の詔は後世作られたものとするのが通説である）。
[[647年]]（大化3）から[[664年]]（天智3）までの間に一括投棄された[[飛鳥京跡|飛鳥京]]の[[木簡]]に「白髪部[[五十戸]]、◎十口」とある。◎は｢師｣の旁が｢皮｣の文字（鍬）｡五十戸を単位として行政的に把握する試みが進められていたことを示している。この統一的造籍、行政的村落把握を実施するには、体系的な法が必要である。[[弘仁格式]]序に「[[天智天皇]]元年に至り、令二十二巻を制す、世人所謂近江朝廷之令也」と伝える。これがいわゆる[[近江令]]である。
明日香の石神遺跡から「（表）乙丑年十二月 三野国ム下評 （裏）大山五十戸造 ム下部知ツ」と記された木簡が出土した。これによって、庚午年籍よりも前の665年に、評里（五十戸）制が施行されており、おそらくは造籍のなされていたであろうことが明らかとなった。常陸国風土記などによれば、立評作業が[[大化]]5年（[[649年]]）と[[白雉]]4年（[[653年]]）に行われており、『日本書紀』白雉3年4月の「是の月、戸籍を造る。凡そ五十戸を里とし、里ごとに長一人」という記事も、一概に捨てがたい。
『[[日本書紀]]』には[[670年]]（天智9）二月条に「戸籍を造り、盗賊と[[浮浪]]とを断ず」とみえる。これが日本で最初の全国的な戸籍で「[[庚午年籍]]」とされる。畿内はもちろん、西は[[九州]]から東は[[常陸国|常陸]]、[[上野国|上野]]まで造籍の実施されたことを示す。[[氏姓制|氏姓]]を確定する台帳の機能を果たしたものと思われる。
「庚午年籍」は現存しておらず、全国的に全ての階層の人民を対象にして造籍したのかどうかも疑われている。つまり、氏や姓を持つ首長や[[豪族]]の民までも把握できたのかということである。しかし、その後の六国史の記事で、かなり下層の人々の改姓訴訟や、あるいは良賤訴訟の際にも、庚午年籍が証拠として参照されている。また、承和6年（839年）には、左右京職并びに五畿内七道諸国に、庚午年籍を写し進ることが命ぜられ、それらが中務省の庫に納められたところをみると、初めての全国（当時の）全階層の戸籍としてよいようである。
== 庚寅年籍 == [[681年]]（天武10）に[[飛鳥浄御原令]]の編纂が開始され、[[689年]]（持統3）になってようやく飛鳥浄御原令ができあがった。その戸令（こりょう）に基づいて[[690年]]（持統4）に全国的な戸籍の庚寅年籍が作成された。人民を地域により編成するという作業はほぼ完了し、[[692年]]（持統2）には、庚寅年籍に基づく[[口分田]]の班給が、畿内で開始された。同時に全国でも[[班田収授法]]が施行されたと推測される。令に則った戸籍を介して個別に人身を把握して、個別人身支配が始まったのである。
この時代の戸籍としては、702年に作成された[[半布里戸籍]]が現存している。
『続日本紀』宝亀10年（779年）6月13日条に「庚午の年より、大宝二年（702年）に至る四比の藉」とあることからも、この年籍以降、六年ごとの造籍がなされるようになったと考えられる。<!--- ？？ --->同戸籍は、その後の六年に一度作成するという「六年一造」の造籍の出発点になっただけでなく、五十戸一里を基準に行政的に[[戸]]を編成して、その戸内の家族（戸口）の名、年齢、戸主との続柄などを詳述したことによって、個々の家族構成を直接的に把握することを可能にし、それを基に班田収授を行い、[[人頭課税]]をする台帳の機能も果たした。また、[[良賤身分]]を定める原簿の機能を付随するようになったようである。
庚寅年籍は現存しないが、2012年、大宰府から、次の戸籍（696年）を作成するための、庚寅年籍以降の異動を記した木簡が出土した。正倉院文書（紙背文書）に含まれる最古の戸籍は702年（大宝2年）のものである（正倉院から流出した文書もある<ref>例 : [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/134337 豊前国仲津郡丁里大宝二年戸籍断簡] 文化遺産オンライン</ref>）。当時の家族形態などを具体的に知ることが出来る。
== 計帳と課役 == 戸籍と同じように、律令時代の人口（人頭）を知ることの出来る史料として、計帳がある。計帳は、[[課役]]を徴収するための基本台帳である。毎年作成された。そこには人口、性別、年齢から一人ひとりの身体的特徴までが[[郷長|里長（郷長）]]によって書き上げられていた。国ごとにまとめられて[[租庸調|調]]（ちょう）、[[租庸調#日本の租庸調|庸]]（よう）、[[雑徭]]（ぞうよう）、[[軍役]]など、課役賦課の基本台帳とされた。
計帳の作成には、三段階あり、三種の文書が出来上がった。一を[[手実]]（しゅじつ）、二を[[歴名]]（れきみょう）、三を[[目録]]という。
手実とは、戸主が作成し、毎年六月末日までに[[京職]]や[[国司]]に提出する申告書である。戸主以下全戸口の姓名・年齢・続柄を書き上げた文書である。当時の識字層からみて、[[郡司]]や里長が手実の作成を代行する場合も多かったであろうと推測される。この手実に基づき、官司では歴名・目録が作成された。
歴名はちょうど[[戸籍]]のように、各戸の手実の内容を一[[郷#日本における郷|里]]（五十戸）分列挙して、一巻の帳簿に編成したものである。各戸ごとに負担すべき調庸額が記録され、おそらく里全体の調庸額も記録され、前年との戸口の異同が詳細に示されていた点が戸籍と異なる。
目録は、具体的な戸の内容を記載していない。数字だけの統計文書である。一国及び各郡の戸数・口数が課役負担の有無を基準として詳細に集計され、前年度との異同、及びその年の調、庸額が示される。[[令]]規定に毎年八月末日までに京に進上するべく義務づけられた計帳とはこの目録を指し、[[京都]]ではこれによって毎年の歳入予定を知るとともに、全国の口数、特に[[課口]]数を掌握していたのである。なお歴名の作成・京に進上の規定は令にはない。
成年男子の21歳から60歳までを[[正丁]]（せいてい）といい、課役の対象となった。
調は、その地域の[[特産物]]で、一定量の[[絹]]、[[絁]]、[[糸]]、[[綿]]、[[布]]などの繊維製品、[[塩]]、[[鰒]]、[[海藻]]、[[堅魚]]などの海産物や[[鉄]]、それに調副物として油、[[染料]]、海産物、山の幸など、様々な物品から成っていた。
庸は、年10日京都へ出て使役される[[歳役]]の代納物である。
しかし[[平安時代]]に差し掛かると、課役を逃れるため虚偽の戸籍が横行した（例：備中国邇磨郷など）。
== 戸籍と紙背文書 == {{main|紙背文書}} 戸籍は令に拠れば30年の保管が行われた後に廃棄されることとなっていたが、当時の[[紙]]は大変貴重品であったために、実際には廃棄されずに他の[[官司]]や[[官寺]]などに回されて、裏面を再利用するのが一般的であった（[[紙背文書]]）。[[東大寺]]写経所の帳簿類を主とする[[正倉院文書]]の中にはこうした戸籍の紙背を再利用したものが含まれている<ref>[[#仁藤|仁藤(2014)]]</ref>。これらは正倉院の宝物そのものとは別の意味で[[歴史学者]]に多くの貴重な情報を提供している。[[半布里戸籍]]も裏面が[[写経]]関係の書類に転用されたため、現代に残ることとなった。
== 終焉 == {{See also|近代以前の日本の人口統計}}戸籍の改製は[[10世紀]]頃まで行われ、[[平安時代|平安]]中期の[[延喜]]年間に編まれた戸籍が現存している。しかし[[三世一身法]]から[[墾田永年私財法]]の制定によって律令制が後退し更に有力貴族や寺社による[[荘園 (日本)|荘園制]]の成立により、戸籍改製の必要性が薄れ全国的な改製が行われなくなった。現存する古代籍帳では、[[寛弘]]元年(1004年)に作成された[[讃岐国]][[大内郡]]入野郷の戸籍が最も時期が遅いものとなっている。但し平安・[[鎌倉時代|鎌倉]]から[[室町時代]]を通じて、荘園内部や[[国衙領]]で戸籍に相当する領民把握が行われていたという見方もある。
== 関連年表 == * 485年（顕宗元）狭狭城山君韓袋宿祢、皇子押磐を殺すことに連坐す。陵戸に充て、籍帳より削除す。 * 540年（欽明元）秦人漢人等の諸蕃で投化せる者を召し集め、国郡に安置して、戸籍に編貫す。秦人の戸数惣じて七千五十三戸。 * 569年（欽明3）[[王辰爾]]の甥の[[白猪胆津|胆津]]を遣わして、[[白猪屯倉]]の田部の丁籍を検定す。胆津に白猪史の姓を賜い、[[田令 (屯倉)|田令]]に任ず。[[葛城山田瑞子|葛城山田直瑞子]]の副とす。 * 574年（敏達3）蘇我馬子大臣を吉備国に遣わして、白猪屯倉と[[田部]]を増益し、田部の名籍を白猪史胆津に授く。 * 645年（大化元）いわゆる東国国司と[[倭の六県]]等に、戸籍を作り、及び田畝を校することを命ず。 * 646年（大化2）改新之詔の其三に「初めて戸籍計帳、班田收授之法を造る。凡そ五十戸を里とし、里ごとに長一人を置く」とある。 * 652年（白雉3）戸籍を造る。凡そ五十戸を里とし。里ごとに長一人。凡そ戸主は皆、家長を以てこれとす。 * 658年（斉明4）この歳、造籍年。 * [[663年]]（天智2）法隆寺の幡に「癸亥年山部五十戸婦」。 * [[664年]]（天智3）この歳、造籍年。冠位階名、及び氏上、民部・家部等の事を定む。この年以前作成の[[飛鳥京跡|飛鳥京出土]][[木簡]]に「白髪部[[五十戸]]、◎十口」。 * [[665年]]（天智4）[[石神遺跡]]出土の木簡に「大山五十戸造」。 * [[670年]]（天智9）戸籍を造り、盗賊と浮浪を断つ。いわゆる庚午年籍。 * [[681年]]（天武10）飛鳥浄御原律令の編纂を開始する。 * [[689年]]（持統3）諸司に令一部二十二巻（[[飛鳥浄御原令]]）をわかつ。八月、戸籍の作成、浮浪人を取り締まる。 * [[690年]]（持統4）九月、戸令により庚寅年籍を造る。 * [[691年]]（持統5）三月、良・賤身分を区別する規定を定める。四月、解放された[[奴婢]]の身分を庚寅年籍で確定する。十月陵戸の制を定める。 * [[692年]]（持統2）畿内で[[口分田]]の班給を開始する。全国的にも行われた。九月、班田大夫を四畿内に遣わす。 * [[693年]]（持統7）一月、百姓に黄色衣、奴に黒衣を着させる。三月、桑・紵・梨・栗などの栽培を奨励し、五穀の助けとする。 * [[694年]]（持統8）[[藤原京]]に都を移す。 * [[697年]]（文武元）[[持統天皇]][[譲位]]し、[[文武天皇]][[即位]]する。 * [[700年]]（文武4）三月、王臣に令文を読習させる。律条を撰定する。諸国の牧地を定め、牛馬を放牧する。六月、刑部親王・[[藤原不比等]]等に、[[大宝律令]]を撰定させる。 * [[701年]]（大宝元）六月、新令によって政治を行う。八月、律令の選定を完成する。[[明法道|明法博士]]を[[西海道]]以外の六道に派遣し、新令を講義させる。 * [[702年]]（大宝2）大宝律を天下にわかつ。七月、新律を講義する。十月、大宝律令を諸国に領下する。十二月、[[持統天皇|持統太上天皇]][[崩御]]する。御野･筑前･豊前・豊後国戸籍が現存。 * [[703年]]（大宝3）庚午年籍を戸籍の原簿とし、改変を禁止する。 * [[706年]]（慶雲3）田租の法を定め、町別十五束（成斤の束）にする。 * [[708年]]（和銅元）陸奥国戸口損益帳現存。 * [[710年]]（和銅3）[[平城京]]に遷都する。 * [[711年]]（和銅4）諸司に律令を励行させる。 * [[713年]]（和銅6）度量・調庸・義倉等に関する五条の事を定める。四月、[[丹後国|丹後]]・[[美作国|美作]]・[[大隅国|大隅]]の三国を建てる。五月、[[郡]]・[[郷]]名に好字をつけさせる。 * [[715年]]（和銅8）五月、逃亡三ヶ月以上の浮浪人に、逃亡地で調庸を課する。 * [[717年]]（霊亀3）里を郷に改め、郷を二・三の里に分ける（郷里制）。四月、[[百姓]]の違法の[[出家]]を禁じ、[[行基]]の活動を禁圧する。五月、大計帳・[[青苗簿]]・[[輸租帳]]などの式を諸国に領下する。五月、[[能登国|能登]]・[[安房国|安房]]・[[石城国|石城]]・[[石背国|石背]]の四国を建てる。国別の衛士数を定める。この年、[[藤原不比等]]らに命じて、律令を撰定する。（[[養老律令]]） * [[719年]]（養老3）天下の民戸に陸田を給する。 * 721年（養老5）下総国戸籍現存。 * 727年（神亀4）造籍年。 * 733年（天平5）造籍年。 * 739年（天平11）造籍年。郷里制廃止。 * 758年（天平宝字2）以後、養老令により造籍。 * 902年（延喜2）阿波国戸籍現存。 * 908年（延喜8）周防国戸籍現存。 * 998年（長徳4）国郡郷未詳戸籍現存。 * 1004年（寛弘元）讃岐国戸籍現存。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group=注釈}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}}

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[古代日本の戸籍制度]] to [[Ancient Japanese Census Registration System]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2011-9}} '''古代日本の戸籍制度'''（こだいにほんのこせきせいど）は、[[飛鳥時代]]に撰定・編纂された[[律令]]による人民把握のための[[戸籍]]。主なものに[[古代日本の戸籍制度#庚午年籍|庚午年籍]]（こうごのねんじゃく）や[[古代日本の戸籍制度#庚寅年籍|庚寅年籍]]（こういんのねんじゃく）があげられる。
[[正倉院文書]]に古代の戸籍の一部が<!---[[死亡帳]]などとして、--->残されている。また近年、[[漆紙文書]]のかたちで[[秋田城|秋田城跡]]や[[多賀城|多賀城跡]]、[[下野国|下野]][[国府]]跡など地方の[[城柵|城柵遺跡]]や[[官衙]]から、戸籍[[木簡]]としては[[周防国|周防]]国府などで出土しており、[[赤外線]]による解読作業がおこなわれている。
このうち庚午年籍は、670年（天智9年／庚午の年）につくられた戸籍。古代においては、一般の戸籍は6年ごとに作成され、30年を経ると廃棄される規定であったが、庚午年籍は永久保存とされた。しかも、この年が『近江令』施行の年でもあったから、これにならってつくられたものである。
==戸籍のはじまり== 造籍に関する古い例としては、
[[540年]]（[[欽明天皇|欽明]]元年）八月の条「秦人（はたひと）・漢人（あやひと）等、諸蕃（となりのくに）より投化せる者を召し集へて、国郡に安置し、戸籍（へのふみた）に編貫す。秦人の戸数七千五十三戸、大蔵掾（おおくらのふびと）を以て、秦伴造（はたのとものみやつこ）となす」（『[[日本書紀]]』）。とあり、欽明朝頃にはまず[[渡来人]]を戸籍によって支配したことが窺われる。
[[569年]]（欽明30）の春正月に、詔で[[吉備国|吉備]]の[[白猪屯倉]]（しらいのみやけ）では、年齢が十歳あまりに達しているのに、籍に漏れているために賦課を免ぜられている者が多い。[[白猪胆津|膽津]]（いつ）を遣わして[[田部]]の丁籍を検定せよと述べた。4月になって、膽津は詔に述べられているとおりによく丁（よほろ）を調査して籍を定め、田戸を編成したので、その功をほめて白猪史（しらいのふひと）の姓を賜い、[[田令 (屯倉)|田令]]（たづかい）に任じた（『日本書紀』）。丁籍は、課役を負担する成年男子のみを記載した。田戸は、田部を編成して丁籍よりも正確な戸籍を造ったのか。はじめに籍を造っただけで、後は定期的に籍を作成することもなかったらしいので、このような不具合が生じたらしい。
さらに[[574年]]（[[敏達天皇|敏達]]3）十月の条に、[[大臣 (古代日本)|大臣]]の[[蘇我馬子]]を吉備に遣わし、白猪屯倉と田部とを増益して、その田部の名籍を膽津に授けたとある（『日本書紀』）。
名籍は、胆津が新しく造ったもので、後の戸籍・計帳に近いものか。これらは渡来系集団や屯倉の田部などの造籍であり、すべての人民を対象とする[[律令制]]の戸籍制とは異なる。
== 庚午年籍 == [[646年]]（大化2）[[改新の詔]]を発布して今後の政治改革の方針を示した（しかし、今日では改新の詔は後世作られたものとするのが通説である）。
[[647年]]（大化3）から[[664年]]（天智3）までの間に一括投棄された[[飛鳥京跡|飛鳥京]]の[[木簡]]に「白髪部[[五十戸]]、◎十口」とある。◎は｢師｣の旁が｢皮｣の文字（鍬）｡五十戸を単位として行政的に把握する試みが進められていたことを示している。この統一的造籍、行政的村落把握を実施するには、体系的な法が必要である。[[弘仁格式]]序に「[[天智天皇]]元年に至り、令二十二巻を制す、世人所謂近江朝廷之令也」と伝える。これがいわゆる[[近江令]]である。
明日香の石神遺跡から「（表）乙丑年十二月 三野国ム下評 （裏）大山五十戸造 ム下部知ツ」と記された木簡が出土した。これによって、庚午年籍よりも前の665年に、評里（五十戸）制が施行されており、おそらくは造籍のなされていたであろうことが明らかとなった。常陸国風土記などによれば、立評作業が[[大化]]5年（[[649年]]）と[[白雉]]4年（[[653年]]）に行われており、『日本書紀』白雉3年4月の「是の月、戸籍を造る。凡そ五十戸を里とし、里ごとに長一人」という記事も、一概に捨てがたい。
『[[日本書紀]]』には[[670年]]（天智9）二月条に「戸籍を造り、盗賊と[[浮浪]]とを断ず」とみえる。これが日本で最初の全国的な戸籍で「[[庚午年籍]]」とされる。畿内はもちろん、西は[[九州]]から東は[[常陸国|常陸]]、[[上野国|上野]]まで造籍の実施されたことを示す。[[氏姓制|氏姓]]を確定する台帳の機能を果たしたものと思われる。
「庚午年籍」は現存しておらず、全国的に全ての階層の人民を対象にして造籍したのかどうかも疑われている。つまり、氏や姓を持つ首長や[[豪族]]の民までも把握できたのかということである。しかし、その後の六国史の記事で、かなり下層の人々の改姓訴訟や、あるいは良賤訴訟の際にも、庚午年籍が証拠として参照されている。また、承和6年（839年）には、左右京職并びに五畿内七道諸国に、庚午年籍を写し進ることが命ぜられ、それらが中務省の庫に納められたところをみると、初めての全国（当時の）全階層の戸籍としてよいようである。
== 庚寅年籍 == [[681年]]（天武10）に[[飛鳥浄御原令]]の編纂が開始され、[[689年]]（持統3）になってようやく飛鳥浄御原令ができあがった。その戸令（こりょう）に基づいて[[690年]]（持統4）に全国的な戸籍の庚寅年籍が作成された。人民を地域により編成するという作業はほぼ完了し、[[692年]]（持統2）には、庚寅年籍に基づく[[口分田]]の班給が、畿内で開始された。同時に全国でも[[班田収授法]]が施行されたと推測される。令に則った戸籍を介して個別に人身を把握して、個別人身支配が始まったのである。
この時代の戸籍としては、702年に作成された[[半布里戸籍]]が現存している。
『続日本紀』宝亀10年（779年）6月13日条に「庚午の年より、大宝二年（702年）に至る四比の藉」とあることからも、この年籍以降、六年ごとの造籍がなされるようになったと考えられる。<!--- ？？ --->同戸籍は、その後の六年に一度作成するという「六年一造」の造籍の出発点になっただけでなく、五十戸一里を基準に行政的に[[戸]]を編成して、その戸内の家族（戸口）の名、年齢、戸主との続柄などを詳述したことによって、個々の家族構成を直接的に把握することを可能にし、それを基に班田収授を行い、[[人頭課税]]をする台帳の機能も果たした。また、[[良賤身分]]を定める原簿の機能を付随するようになったようである。
庚寅年籍は現存しないが、2012年、大宰府から、次の戸籍（696年）を作成するための、庚寅年籍以降の異動を記した木簡が出土した。正倉院文書（紙背文書）に含まれる最古の戸籍は702年（大宝2年）のものである（正倉院から流出した文書もある<ref>例 : [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/134337 豊前国仲津郡丁里大宝二年戸籍断簡] 文化遺産オンライン</ref>）。当時の家族形態などを具体的に知ることが出来る。
== 計帳と課役 == 戸籍と同じように、律令時代の人口（人頭）を知ることの出来る史料として、計帳がある。計帳は、[[課役]]を徴収するための基本台帳である。毎年作成された。そこには人口、性別、年齢から一人ひとりの身体的特徴までが[[郷長|里長（郷長）]]によって書き上げられていた。国ごとにまとめられて[[租庸調|調]]（ちょう）、[[租庸調#日本の租庸調|庸]]（よう）、[[雑徭]]（ぞうよう）、[[軍役]]など、課役賦課の基本台帳とされた。
計帳の作成には、三段階あり、三種の文書が出来上がった。一を[[手実]]（しゅじつ）、二を[[歴名]]（れきみょう）、三を[[目録]]という。
手実とは、戸主が作成し、毎年六月末日までに[[京職]]や[[国司]]に提出する申告書である。戸主以下全戸口の姓名・年齢・続柄を書き上げた文書である。当時の識字層からみて、[[郡司]]や里長が手実の作成を代行する場合も多かったであろうと推測される。この手実に基づき、官司では歴名・目録が作成された。
歴名はちょうど[[戸籍]]のように、各戸の手実の内容を一[[郷#日本における郷|里]]（五十戸）分列挙して、一巻の帳簿に編成したものである。各戸ごとに負担すべき調庸額が記録され、おそらく里全体の調庸額も記録され、前年との戸口の異同が詳細に示されていた点が戸籍と異なる。
目録は、具体的な戸の内容を記載していない。数字だけの統計文書である。一国及び各郡の戸数・口数が課役負担の有無を基準として詳細に集計され、前年度との異同、及びその年の調、庸額が示される。[[令]]規定に毎年八月末日までに京に進上するべく義務づけられた計帳とはこの目録を指し、[[京都]]ではこれによって毎年の歳入予定を知るとともに、全国の口数、特に[[課口]]数を掌握していたのである。なお歴名の作成・京に進上の規定は令にはない。
成年男子の21歳から60歳までを[[正丁]]（せいてい）といい、課役の対象となった。
調は、その地域の[[特産物]]で、一定量の[[絹]]、[[絁]]、[[糸]]、[[綿]]、[[布]]などの繊維製品、[[塩]]、[[鰒]]、[[海藻]]、[[堅魚]]などの海産物や[[鉄]]、それに調副物として油、[[染料]]、海産物、山の幸など、様々な物品から成っていた。
庸は、年10日京都へ出て使役される[[歳役]]の代納物である。
しかし[[平安時代]]に差し掛かると、課役を逃れるため虚偽の戸籍が横行した（例：備中国邇磨郷など）。
== 戸籍と紙背文書 == {{main|紙背文書}} 戸籍は令に拠れば30年の保管が行われた後に廃棄されることとなっていたが、当時の[[紙]]は大変貴重品であったために、実際には廃棄されずに他の[[官司]]や[[官寺]]などに回されて、裏面を再利用するのが一般的であった（[[紙背文書]]）。[[東大寺]]写経所の帳簿類を主とする[[正倉院文書]]の中にはこうした戸籍の紙背を再利用したものが含まれている<ref>[[#仁藤|仁藤(2014)]]</ref>。これらは正倉院の宝物そのものとは別の意味で[[歴史学者]]に多くの貴重な情報を提供している。[[半布里戸籍]]も裏面が[[写経]]関係の書類に転用されたため、現代に残ることとなった。
== 終焉 == {{See also|近代以前の日本の人口統計}}戸籍の改製は[[10世紀]]頃まで行われ、[[平安時代|平安]]中期の[[延喜]]年間に編まれた戸籍が現存している。しかし[[三世一身法]]から[[墾田永年私財法]]の制定によって律令制が後退し更に有力貴族や寺社による[[荘園 (日本)|荘園制]]の成立により、戸籍改製の必要性が薄れ全国的な改製が行われなくなった。現存する古代籍帳では、[[寛弘]]元年(1004年)に作成された[[讃岐国]][[大内郡]]入野郷の戸籍が最も時期が遅いものとなっている。但し平安・[[鎌倉時代|鎌倉]]から[[室町時代]]を通じて、荘園内部や[[国衙領]]で戸籍に相当する領民把握が行われていたという見方もある。
== 関連年表 == * 485年（顕宗元）狭狭城山君韓袋宿祢、皇子押磐を殺すことに連坐す。陵戸に充て、籍帳より削除す。 * 540年（欽明元）秦人漢人等の諸蕃で投化せる者を召し集め、国郡に安置して、戸籍に編貫す。秦人の戸数惣じて七千五十三戸。 * 569年（欽明3）[[王辰爾]]の甥の[[白猪胆津|胆津]]を遣わして、[[白猪屯倉]]の田部の丁籍を検定す。胆津に白猪史の姓を賜い、[[田令 (屯倉)|田令]]に任ず。[[葛城山田瑞子|葛城山田直瑞子]]の副とす。 * 574年（敏達3）蘇我馬子大臣を吉備国に遣わして、白猪屯倉と[[田部]]を増益し、田部の名籍を白猪史胆津に授く。 * 645年（大化元）いわゆる東国国司と[[倭の六県]]等に、戸籍を作り、及び田畝を校することを命ず。 * 646年（大化2）改新之詔の其三に「初めて戸籍計帳、班田收授之法を造る。凡そ五十戸を里とし、里ごとに長一人を置く」とある。 * 652年（白雉3）戸籍を造る。凡そ五十戸を里とし。里ごとに長一人。凡そ戸主は皆、家長を以てこれとす。 * 658年（斉明4）この歳、造籍年。 * [[663年]]（天智2）法隆寺の幡に「癸亥年山部五十戸婦」。 * [[664年]]（天智3）この歳、造籍年。冠位階名、及び氏上、民部・家部等の事を定む。この年以前作成の[[飛鳥京跡|飛鳥京出土]][[木簡]]に「白髪部[[五十戸]]、◎十口」。 * [[665年]]（天智4）[[石神遺跡]]出土の木簡に「大山五十戸造」。 * [[670年]]（天智9）戸籍を造り、盗賊と浮浪を断つ。いわゆる庚午年籍。 * [[681年]]（天武10）飛鳥浄御原律令の編纂を開始する。 * [[689年]]（持統3）諸司に令一部二十二巻（[[飛鳥浄御原令]]）をわかつ。八月、戸籍の作成、浮浪人を取り締まる。 * [[690年]]（持統4）九月、戸令により庚寅年籍を造る。 * [[691年]]（持統5）三月、良・賤身分を区別する規定を定める。四月、解放された[[奴婢]]の身分を庚寅年籍で確定する。十月陵戸の制を定める。 * [[692年]]（持統2）畿内で[[口分田]]の班給を開始する。全国的にも行われた。九月、班田大夫を四畿内に遣わす。 * [[693年]]（持統7）一月、百姓に黄色衣、奴に黒衣を着させる。三月、桑・紵・梨・栗などの栽培を奨励し、五穀の助けとする。 * [[694年]]（持統8）[[藤原京]]に都を移す。 * [[697年]]（文武元）[[持統天皇]][[譲位]]し、[[文武天皇]][[即位]]する。 * [[700年]]（文武4）三月、王臣に令文を読習させる。律条を撰定する。諸国の牧地を定め、牛馬を放牧する。六月、刑部親王・[[藤原不比等]]等に、[[大宝律令]]を撰定させる。 * [[701年]]（大宝元）六月、新令によって政治を行う。八月、律令の選定を完成する。[[明法道|明法博士]]を[[西海道]]以外の六道に派遣し、新令を講義させる。 * [[702年]]（大宝2）大宝律を天下にわかつ。七月、新律を講義する。十月、大宝律令を諸国に領下する。十二月、[[持統天皇|持統太上天皇]][[崩御]]する。御野･筑前･豊前・豊後国戸籍が現存。 * [[703年]]（大宝3）庚午年籍を戸籍の原簿とし、改変を禁止する。 * [[706年]]（慶雲3）田租の法を定め、町別十五束（成斤の束）にする。 * [[708年]]（和銅元）陸奥国戸口損益帳現存。 * [[710年]]（和銅3）[[平城京]]に遷都する。 * [[711年]]（和銅4）諸司に律令を励行させる。 * [[713年]]（和銅6）度量・調庸・義倉等に関する五条の事を定める。四月、[[丹後国|丹後]]・[[美作国|美作]]・[[大隅国|大隅]]の三国を建てる。五月、[[郡]]・[[郷]]名に好字をつけさせる。 * [[715年]]（和銅8）五月、逃亡三ヶ月以上の浮浪人に、逃亡地で調庸を課する。 * [[717年]]（霊亀3）里を郷に改め、郷を二・三の里に分ける（郷里制）。四月、[[百姓]]の違法の[[出家]]を禁じ、[[行基]]の活動を禁圧する。五月、大計帳・[[青苗簿]]・[[輸租帳]]などの式を諸国に領下する。五月、[[能登国|能登]]・[[安房国|安房]]・[[石城国|石城]]・[[石背国|石背]]の四国を建てる。国別の衛士数を定める。この年、[[藤原不比等]]らに命じて、律令を撰定する。（[[養老律令]]） * [[719年]]（養老3）天下の民戸に陸田を給する。 * 721年（養老5）下総国戸籍現存。 * 727年（神亀4）造籍年。 * 733年（天平5）造籍年。 * 739年（天平11）造籍年。郷里制廃止。 * 758年（天平宝字2）以後、養老令により造籍。 * 902年（延喜2）阿波国戸籍現存。 * 908年（延喜8）周防国戸籍現存。 * 998年（長徳4）国郡郷未詳戸籍現存。 * 1004年（寛弘元）讃岐国戸籍現存。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group=注釈}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * {{Cite journal|和書|author=仁藤敦史 |title=正倉院文書研究と歴博複製事業の役割 (人間文化研究機構連携研究 正倉院文書の高度情報化研究) |journal=国立歴史民俗博物館研究報告 |ISSN=0286-7400 |publisher=国立歴史民俗博物館 |date=2014-12 |volume=192 |pages=57-64 |naid=120005749094 |doi=10.15024/00002189 |url=https://doi.org/10.15024/00002189 |ref=仁藤}}
== 関連項目 == * [[律令制]] * [[古代日本の地方官制]] * [[租庸調]]・[[雑徭]] * [[軍団 (古代日本)]] * [[本貫]] * [[計帳]] * [[死亡帳]] * [[隠首]] * [[括出]] * [[富加町#名所・旧跡・遺跡・観光スポット|富加町]] * [[近代以前の日本の人口統計]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fa:سیستم ثبت خانواده در ژاپن باستان]][[ja:古代日本の戸籍制度]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11410913]] Wikidata name: koseki system in classical Japan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:36, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11410913]] Wikidata name: koseki system in classical Japan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ancient Japanese Census Registration System''' (古代日本の戸籍制度, Kodai Nihon no Koseki Seido) refers to the census registration system established during the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]] in the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] for population management. Notable examples include the {{ill|Kōgo Census|ja|庚午年籍|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōgo Census|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 古代日本の戸籍制度|1=koseki system in classical Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11410913}} (670 AD) and the {{ill|Kōin Census|ja|庚寅年籍|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōin Census|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 古代日本の戸籍制度|1=koseki system in classical Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11410913}} (690 AD). Some portions of these ancient census records survive as part of the {{ill|Shōsōin documents|ja|正倉院文書|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōsōin documents|qq=|1=Shōsōin Documents|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545362}}, while other records have been uncovered as {{ill|lacquered paper documents|ja|漆紙文書|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lacquered paper documents|qq=|1=urushigami monjo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11565404}} at sites such as the {{ill|Akita Castle|ja|秋田城跡|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Castle|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 秋田城|1=Akita Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11595283}} and {{ill|Tagajō|ja|多賀城跡|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagajō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 多賀城|1=Taga Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q870867}}, as well as in the form of wooden tablets (木簡) in provincial locations like the [[:en:Suō Province|Suō Province]] capital. Advances in infrared analysis have facilitated the deciphering of these records. The Kōgo Census, compiled in 670 AD (the 9th year of Emperor Tenji's reign), marked Japan's first nationwide census. Although census records were typically destroyed after 30 years, the Kōgo Census was designated for permanent preservation. This census coincided with the enforcement of the [[:en:Ōmi Code|Ōmi Code]], reflecting its legal framework. == The Beginnings of Census Records == Early mentions of census compilation appear in: * '''540 AD (Emperor Kinmei's reign):''' "Foreigners such as Qin and Han people, settled in the regions and listed in census registers." This indicates that census registration initially focused on managing immigrant populations.<ref>[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]</ref> * '''569 AD:''' Emperor Kinmei ordered inspections of tax registries in the {{ill|Shirai no Miyake|ja|白猪屯倉|1=Shirai no Miyake|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirai no Miyake|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirai no Miyake|qq=}} in the [[:en:Kibi Province|Kibi Province]] to ensure proper registration of taxable individuals. * '''574 AD:''' [[:en:Soga no Umako|Soga no Umako]] increased census efforts in Kibi, refining population control in granary-associated labor. These efforts primarily targeted immigrants and administrative groups such as granary laborers, differing from the comprehensive population control established under the Ritsuryō system. == Kōgo Census == The {{ill|Kōgo Census|ja|庚午年籍|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōgo Census|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 古代日本の戸籍制度|1=koseki system in classical Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11410913}} was compiled in 670 AD to implement the reforms proposed by the [[:en:Taika Reform|Taika Reforms]]. It represented Japan's first comprehensive national census, covering areas from [[:en:Kyushu|Kyūshū]] to {{ill|Kantō|ja|関東|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō|qq=|1=Kantō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655841}}. However, the original records are no longer extant, and questions remain about whether all societal classes were included. Subsequent records and lawsuits involving surname changes or social status disputes, however, demonstrate its use as a foundational document. The Kōgo Census was copied and stored in the central government archives until the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]. == Kōin Census == The {{ill|Kōin Census|ja|庚寅年籍|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōin Census|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 古代日本の戸籍制度|1=koseki system in classical Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11410913}}, compiled in 690 AD under the [[:en:Asuka Kiyomihara Code|Asuka Kiyomihara Code]], represented the next stage in census development. It detailed all households and individuals, enabling the implementation of the {{ill|Handen-Shūju System|ja|班田収授法|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Handen-Shūju System|qq=|1=Handen-Shūju|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8192257}} and comprehensive tax collection. == Census Records and Reused Documents == Due to the high value of paper in ancient Japan, discarded census records were often reused by temples and government offices for other purposes. Many such reused documents, now known as {{ill|Recycled Paper Documents|ja|紙背文書|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Recycled Paper Documents|qq=|1=shihai monjo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605537}}, have been preserved in collections such as the [[:en:Shōsōin|Shōsōin]]. == Conclusion == The comprehensive compilation of census records continued until the mid-10th century but declined as the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]] weakened. The establishment of the {{ill|Shōen system|ja|荘園制|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōen system|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荘園|1=manorialism|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1550557}} reduced the need for centralized population management, marking the end of systematic census compilation. == Related Chronology == - 540 AD: Census registration of immigrant groups. - 670 AD: Compilation of the Kōgo Census. - 690 AD: Compilation of the Kōin Census under the Asuka Kiyomihara Code. - 702 AD: The oldest surviving census, the {{ill|Hanpurī Census|ja|半布里戸籍|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanpurī Census|qq=|1=Hanpurī Census|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11407005}}, was compiled. == References == <references/> == Further Reading == * {{Cite journal|author=Atsushi Nito |title=Research on Shōsōin Documents |journal=National Museum of Japanese History Bulletin |date=2014-12 |volume=192 |pages=57–64 |url=https://doi.org/10.15024/00002189|qq=}} == See Also == * [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]] * {{ill|Handen-Shūju System|ja|班田収授法|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Handen-Shūju System|qq=|1=Handen-Shūju|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8192257}} * {{ill|Ancient Japanese Provincial Administration|ja|古代日本の地方官制|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Japanese Provincial Administration|qq=|1=local government in classical Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11410912}} * {{ill|Census Records|ja|計帳|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Census Records|qq=|1=Census Records|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11631032}}
{{translated page|ja|古代日本の戸籍制度|version=103916794|comment=Imported full ja history}}

[[Category:Asuka period]] [[Category:Nara period]]
}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fa:سیستم ثبت خانواده در ژاپن باستان]][[ja:古代日本の戸籍制度]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11410913]] Wikidata name: koseki system in classical Japan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:36, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11410913]] Wikidata name: koseki system in classical Japan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ancient Japanese Census Registration System''' (古代日本の戸籍制度, Kodai Nihon no Koseki Seido) refers to the census registration system established during the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]] in the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] for population management. Notable examples include the {{ill|Kōgo Census|ja|庚午年籍|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōgo Census|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 古代日本の戸籍制度|1=koseki system in classical Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11410913}} (670 AD) and the {{ill|Kōin Census|ja|庚寅年籍|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōin Census|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 古代日本の戸籍制度|1=koseki system in classical Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11410913}} (690 AD). Some portions of these ancient census records survive as part of the {{ill|Shōsōin documents|ja|正倉院文書|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōsōin documents|1=Shōsōin Documents|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545362}}, while other records have been uncovered as {{ill|lacquered paper documents|ja|漆紙文書|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lacquered paper documents|1=urushigami monjo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11565404}} at sites such as the {{ill|Akita Castle|ja|秋田城跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Castle|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 秋田城|1=Akita Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11595283}} and {{ill|Tagajō|ja|多賀城跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagajō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 多賀城|1=Taga Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q870867}}, as well as in the form of wooden tablets (木簡) in provincial locations like the [[:en:Suō Province|Suō Province]] capital. Advances in infrared analysis have facilitated the deciphering of these records. The Kōgo Census, compiled in 670 AD (the 9th year of Emperor Tenji's reign), marked Japan's first nationwide census. Although census records were typically destroyed after 30 years, the Kōgo Census was designated for permanent preservation. This census coincided with the enforcement of the [[:en:Ōmi Code|Ōmi Code]], reflecting its legal framework. == The Beginnings of Census Records == Early mentions of census compilation appear in: * '''540 AD (Emperor Kinmei's reign):''' "Foreigners such as Qin and Han people, settled in the regions and listed in census registers." This indicates that census registration initially focused on managing immigrant populations.<ref>[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]</ref> * '''569 AD:''' Emperor Kinmei ordered inspections of tax registries in the {{ill|Shirai no Miyake|ja|白猪屯倉|1=Shirai no Miyake|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirai no Miyake|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirai no Miyake|qq=}} in the [[:en:Kibi Province|Kibi Province]] to ensure proper registration of taxable individuals. * '''574 AD:''' [[:en:Soga no Umako|Soga no Umako]] increased census efforts in Kibi, refining population control in granary-associated labor. These efforts primarily targeted immigrants and administrative groups such as granary laborers, differing from the comprehensive population control established under the Ritsuryō system. == Kōgo Census == The {{ill|Kōgo Census|ja|庚午年籍|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōgo Census|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 古代日本の戸籍制度|1=koseki system in classical Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11410913}} was compiled in 670 AD to implement the reforms proposed by the [[:en:Taika Reform|Taika Reforms]]. It represented Japan's first comprehensive national census, covering areas from [[:en:Kyushu|Kyūshū]] to {{ill|Kantō|ja|関東|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō|1=Kantō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655841}}. However, the original records are no longer extant, and questions remain about whether all societal classes were included. Subsequent records and lawsuits involving surname changes or social status disputes, however, demonstrate its use as a foundational document. The Kōgo Census was copied and stored in the central government archives until the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]. == Kōin Census == The {{ill|Kōin Census|ja|庚寅年籍|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōin Census|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 古代日本の戸籍制度|1=koseki system in classical Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11410913}}, compiled in 690 AD under the [[:en:Asuka Kiyomihara Code|Asuka Kiyomihara Code]], represented the next stage in census development. It detailed all households and individuals, enabling the implementation of the {{ill|Handen-Shūju System|ja|班田収授法|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Handen-Shūju System|1=Handen-Shūju|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8192257}} and comprehensive tax collection. == Census Records and Reused Documents == Due to the high value of paper in ancient Japan, discarded census records were often reused by temples and government offices for other purposes. Many such reused documents, now known as {{ill|Recycled Paper Documents|ja|紙背文書|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Recycled Paper Documents|1=shihai monjo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605537}}, have been preserved in collections such as the [[:en:Shōsōin|Shōsōin]]. == Conclusion == The comprehensive compilation of census records continued until the mid-10th century but declined as the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]] weakened. The establishment of the {{ill|Shōen system|ja|荘園制|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōen system|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荘園|1=manorialism|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1550557}} reduced the need for centralized population management, marking the end of systematic census compilation. == Related Chronology == - 540 AD: Census registration of immigrant groups. - 670 AD: Compilation of the Kōgo Census. - 690 AD: Compilation of the Kōin Census under the Asuka Kiyomihara Code. - 702 AD: The oldest surviving census, the {{ill|Hanpurī Census|ja|半布里戸籍|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanpurī Census|1=Hanpurī Census|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11407005}}, was compiled. == References == <references/> == Further Reading == * {{Cite journal|author=Atsushi Nito |title=Research on Shōsōin Documents |journal=National Museum of Japanese History Bulletin |date=2014-12 |volume=192 |pages=57–64 |url=https://doi.org/10.15024/00002189|qq=}} == See Also == * [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]] * {{ill|Handen-Shūju System|ja|班田収授法|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Handen-Shūju System|1=Handen-Shūju|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8192257}} * {{ill|Ancient Japanese Provincial Administration|ja|古代日本の地方官制|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Japanese Provincial Administration|1=local government in classical Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11410912}} * {{ill|Census Records|ja|計帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Census Records|1=Census Records|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11631032}}

<!--Ancient Japanese Census Registration System--><!--Ancient Japanese Census Registration System--><!--Ancient Japanese Census Registration System--> [[Category:Family registers]] [[Category:Ritsuryō System of Japan]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[fa:سیستم ثبت خانواده در ژاپن باستان]] {{wikidata link|Q11410913}} [[Category:Pages linked to by wikidata]] [[Category:Non matching pagename on wikidata]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fa:سیستم ثبت خانواده در ژاپن باستان]][[ja:古代日本の戸籍制度]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11410913]] Wikidata name: koseki system in classical Japan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:36, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11410913]] Wikidata name: koseki system in classical Japan [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ancient Japanese Census Registration System''' (古代日本の戸籍制度, Kodai Nihon no Koseki Seido) refers to the census registration system established during the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]] in the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] for population management. Notable examples include the {{ill|Kōgo Census|ja|庚午年籍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōgo Census|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 古代日本の戸籍制度|1=koseki system in classical Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11410913}} (670 AD) and the {{ill|Kōin Census|ja|庚寅年籍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōin Census|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 古代日本の戸籍制度|1=koseki system in classical Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11410913}} (690 AD). Some portions of these ancient census records survive as part of the {{ill|Shōsōin documents|ja|正倉院文書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōsōin documents|1=Shōsōin Documents|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545362}}, while other records have been uncovered as {{ill|lacquered paper documents|ja|漆紙文書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lacquered paper documents|1=urushigami monjo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11565404}} at sites such as the {{ill|Akita Castle|ja|秋田城跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akita Castle|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 秋田城|1=Akita Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11595283}} and {{ill|Tagajō|ja|多賀城跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagajō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 多賀城|1=Taga Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q870867}}, as well as in the form of wooden tablets (木簡) in provincial locations like the [[:en:Suō Province|Suō Province]] capital. Advances in infrared analysis have facilitated the deciphering of these records. The Kōgo Census, compiled in 670 AD (the 9th year of Emperor Tenji's reign), marked Japan's first nationwide census. Although census records were typically destroyed after 30 years, the Kōgo Census was designated for permanent preservation. This census coincided with the enforcement of the [[:en:Ōmi Code|Ōmi Code]], reflecting its legal framework. == The Beginnings of Census Records == Early mentions of census compilation appear in: * '''540 AD (Emperor Kinmei's reign):''' "Foreigners such as Qin and Han people, settled in the regions and listed in census registers." This indicates that census registration initially focused on managing immigrant populations.<ref>[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]</ref> * '''569 AD:''' Emperor Kinmei ordered inspections of tax registries in the {{ill|Shirai no Miyake|ja|白猪屯倉|1=Shirai no Miyake|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirai no Miyake|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirai no Miyake|qq=}} in the [[:en:Kibi Province|Kibi Province]] to ensure proper registration of taxable individuals. * '''574 AD:''' [[:en:Soga no Umako|Soga no Umako]] increased census efforts in Kibi, refining population control in granary-associated labor. These efforts primarily targeted immigrants and administrative groups such as granary laborers, differing from the comprehensive population control established under the Ritsuryō system. == Kōgo Census == The {{ill|Kōgo Census|ja|庚午年籍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōgo Census|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 古代日本の戸籍制度|1=koseki system in classical Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11410913}} was compiled in 670 AD to implement the reforms proposed by the [[:en:Taika Reform|Taika Reforms]]. It represented Japan's first comprehensive national census, covering areas from [[:en:Kyushu|Kyūshū]] to {{ill|Kantō|ja|関東|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō|1=Kantō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11655841}}. However, the original records are no longer extant, and questions remain about whether all societal classes were included. Subsequent records and lawsuits involving surname changes or social status disputes, however, demonstrate its use as a foundational document. The Kōgo Census was copied and stored in the central government archives until the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]. == Kōin Census == The {{ill|Kōin Census|ja|庚寅年籍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōin Census|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 古代日本の戸籍制度|1=koseki system in classical Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11410913}}, compiled in 690 AD under the [[:en:Asuka Kiyomihara Code|Asuka Kiyomihara Code]], represented the next stage in census development. It detailed all households and individuals, enabling the implementation of the {{ill|Handen-Shūju System|ja|班田収授法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Handen-Shūju System|1=Handen-Shūju|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8192257}} and comprehensive tax collection. == Census Records and Reused Documents == Due to the high value of paper in ancient Japan, discarded census records were often reused by temples and government offices for other purposes. Many such reused documents, now known as {{ill|Recycled Paper Documents|ja|紙背文書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Recycled Paper Documents|1=shihai monjo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605537}}, have been preserved in collections such as the [[:en:Shōsōin|Shōsōin]]. == Conclusion == The comprehensive compilation of census records continued until the mid-10th century but declined as the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]] weakened. The establishment of the {{ill|Shōen system|ja|荘園制|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōen system|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荘園|1=manorialism|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1550557}} reduced the need for centralized population management, marking the end of systematic census compilation. == Related Chronology == - 540 AD: Census registration of immigrant groups. - 670 AD: Compilation of the Kōgo Census. - 690 AD: Compilation of the Kōin Census under the Asuka Kiyomihara Code. - 702 AD: The oldest surviving census, the {{ill|Hanpurī Census|ja|半布里戸籍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanpurī Census|1=Hanpurī Census|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11407005}}, was compiled. == References == <references/> == Further Reading == * {{Cite journal|author=Atsushi Nito |title=Research on Shōsōin Documents |journal=National Museum of Japanese History Bulletin |date=2014-12 |volume=192 |pages=57–64 |url=https://doi.org/10.15024/00002189|qq=}} == See Also == * [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]] * {{ill|Handen-Shūju System|ja|班田収授法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Handen-Shūju System|1=Handen-Shūju|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8192257}} * {{ill|Ancient Japanese Provincial Administration|ja|古代日本の地方官制|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Japanese Provincial Administration|1=local government in classical Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11410912}} * {{ill|Census Records|ja|計帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Census Records|1=Census Records|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11631032}}
{{translated page|ja|古代日本の戸籍制度|version=103916794|comment=Imported full ja history}}
